# Inox

> Self-hosted, air-gapped OpenAPI SDK generator. One OpenAPI spec compiles into idiomatic SDKs for six languages (TypeScript, Python, Go, Java, Ruby, C#) plus an MCP server and a CLI. Zero runtime dependencies, verified on every build.

Inox is the open, self-hostable alternative to Stainless (acquired by Anthropic and wound down, May 2026), Speakeasy, and Fern. It auto-derives resources, methods, schemas, pagination, and auth directly from the spec — you only declare package names, the base URL, and the auth scheme. It was tested on 12 public production specs (Stripe, OpenAI, DigitalOcean, Plaid, Box, Asana, Discord, Twilio, Adyen, Ory, SendGrid, GitHub); 11 of 12 generate TypeScript SDKs that compile with zero errors and no per-spec tuning.

## Docs
- [README](https://github.com/CREVIOS/inox/blob/main/README.md): overview, install, commands, comparison
- [Landing site](https://crevios.github.io/inox/): features, proof table, capability matrix
- [Reproducible benchmark](https://github.com/CREVIOS/inox/tree/main/benchmarks): run the spec suite yourself

## Install
- npm: `npx @crevious/inox --help` or `npm install -g @crevious/inox`
- bash: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/CREVIOS/inox/main/install.sh | bash`
- docker: `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" inox generate --out sdk`

## Commands
- `inox init` — scaffold a sample sdkgen.yml + openapi.yaml
- `inox generate` — generate SDKs for all targets (or `--target typescript|python|go|java|ruby|csharp`)
- `inox verify` — compile + run endpoint conformance for every language
- `inox products` — generate docs, CLI, an MCP server, a Terraform provider, CI automation
- `inox mcp` — run the generator itself as an MCP server so agents can drive generation
- `inox lint`, `inox diff`, `inox release`, `inox sbom`, `inox studio`

## Key facts
- Languages: TypeScript, Python, Go, Java, Ruby, C#
- Generated runtime has zero third-party dependencies in every language
- MCP server per API (protocol 2025-06-18) with typed / dynamic / code tool modes and read-only scoping
- Supply chain: CycloneDX SBOM + SLSA provenance + OIDC trusted publishing generated by default
- Runtime endpoint conformance across all six languages on every build
- License: MIT. Runs fully offline; your spec never leaves your machine.
